hooks.command: INPUT=$(cat); ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$ROOT" ] && exit 0; FILE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); [ -z "$FILE" ] && exit 0; case "$FILE" in "$ROOT"|"$ROOT"/*) exit 0 ;; /*) echo "{\"hookSpecificOutput\":{\"hookEventName\":\"PreToolUse\",\"permissionDecision\":\"deny\",\"permissionDecisionReason\":\"Path outside project root: $FILE\"}}";; esac

INPUT=$(cat); ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$ROOT" ] && exit 0; CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); [ -z "$CMD" ] && exit 0; FPATH=$(echo "$CMD" | grep -oP 'find +\K/[^ |;&"\x27]*' | head -1); [ -z "$FPATH" ] && exit 0; case "$FPATH" in "$ROOT"|"$ROOT"/*) exit 0 ;; /*) echo "{\"hookSpecificOutput\":{\"hookEventName\":\"PreToolUse\",\"permissionDecision\":\"deny\",\"permissionDecisionReason\":\"find searches outside project root: $FPATH\"}}";; esac

INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); [ -z "$CMD" ] && exit 0; if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE '(^|[ ;&|`(])ssh([ ]|$)' && echo "$CMD" | grep -qP '(sed[ \x22\x27\x60]+-i|[ \x22\x27\x60]vi[ \x22\x27\x60]|[ \x22\x27\x60]vim[ \x22\x27\x60]|[ \x22\x27\x60]nano[ \x22\x27\x60]|[ \x22\x27\x60]tee[ \x22\x27\x60]|>[ ]*[^ &|;>]+)'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"ssh + direct content edit on a remote system (sed -i, vi/vim/nano, tee, > redirect). Repo-owned files (code, schemas, configs in git) must change via plan -> coder -> reviewer -> PR -> deployment, never via in-place edit. See WORKFLOW.md remote-system boundary rule."}}'; fi

INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); [ -z "$CMD" ] && exit 0; if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE '(^|[ ;&|`(])ssh([ ]|$)' && echo "$CMD" | grep -qP '([ \x22\x27\x60]systemctl[ \x22\x27\x60]+(restart|reload|stop|start|enable|disable)|[ \x22\x27\x60]docker[ \x22\x27\x60]+(exec|compose[ \x22\x27\x60]+(up|down|run|restart|exec))|[ \x22\x27\x60]apt(-get)?[ \x22\x27\x60]+(install|upgrade|remove|purge|autoremove)|[ \x22\x27\x60]npm[ \x22\x27\x60]+(install|update|uninstall)|[ \x22\x27\x60]kubectl[ \x22\x27\x60]+(edit|apply|patch|delete|create|replace)|[ \x22\x27\x60]rm[ \x22\x27\x60]|[ \x22\x27\x60]cp[ \x22\x27\x60]|[ \x22\x27\x60]mv[ \x22\x27\x60]|[ \x22\x27\x60]mkdir[ \x22\x27\x60]|[ \x22\x27\x60]touch[ \x22\x27\x60])'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"ask","permissionDecisionReason":"ssh + mutating action on remote system (service restart, container/package management, file ops). Legitimate when it is part of deployment, PM-initiated maintenance, or runtime-state operation that does not live in the repo. Confirm intent before proceeding. If you are editing a repo-owned file in place, stop and go through plan -> coder -> reviewer -> PR."}}'; fi

INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); [ -z "$CMD" ] && exit 0; if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git[ ]+push([ ]+[^ ]+)*[ ]+(--force|--force-with-lease|-f)([ =]|$)'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"ask","permissionDecisionReason":"git push --force / -f rewrites remote history. Confirm with PM that this is intentional (e.g., rewriting a feature branch they own); never force-push to main."}}'; fi

INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); [ -z "$CMD" ] && exit 0; if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git[ ]+commit([ ]+[^ ]+)*[ ]+(--no-verify|--no-gpg-sign)([ =]|$)'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"ask","permissionDecisionReason":"git commit --no-verify / --no-gpg-sign skips pre-commit hooks or signing. Confirm with PM that this is intentional. If a pre-commit hook is failing, fix the underlying issue rather than bypassing the hook."}}'; fi

INPUT=$(cat); NAME=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.subagent_type // .tool_input.skill // empty'); [ -z "$NAME" ] && exit 0; case "$NAME" in wb-development:coder|wb-development:code-reviewer|wb-development:design-review|wb-development:plan-feature|wb-development:pr-prep|wb-git:workflow|wb-git:pr-author) jq -nc --arg n "$NAME" '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"PreToolUse",permissionDecision:"deny",permissionDecisionReason:("\($n) duplicates a protocol role in this ai-pm project. Use the pm-* equivalent (pm-coder / pm-plan-checker + code-review / pm-architect / /pm-plan / pm-pr-prep / WORKFLOW.md git rules), not a wb-* role agent. code-review, deep-research and wb-knowledge skills stay available.")}}' ;; esac

INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); [ -z "$FILE" ] && exit 0; CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.content // empty'); STRIPPED=$(printf '%s' "$CONTENT" | tr -d ' \t\n\r'); [ -n "$STRIPPED" ] && exit 0; [ ! -s "$FILE" ] && exit 0; echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"Empty/whitespace-only Write over an existing non-empty file would zero authored content (truncation guard, mirrors the code-scalpel SC6 invariant: write rejects empty content). This is never a legitimate need -- a genuine truncate should use a different tool, and additions should use Edit. Regression guard for the 2026-06-06 doc-loss incident."}}'

INPUT=$(cat); P=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.prompt // empty'); printf '%s' "$P" | grep -qiE 'implement|refactor|feature|deploy|release|\bfix\b|\badd\b|\bchange\b|\bbuild\b|\bcommit\b|\bmerge\b|\bbug\b|\bremove\b|\bdelete\b|\bdrop\b|\brename\b|\bextract\b|\bupdate\b|сдела|делай|поправ|добав|исправ|реализ|почини|зарелиз|закоммит|смерж|внедри|перепиш|рефактор|фич|убери|убрать|удали|сними|вынеси|переименуй|обнови' || exit 0; jq -nc '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"UserPromptSubmit",additionalContext:"[ai-pm protocol] Repo-change request detected. Follow WORKFLOW.md: Step 0 git/branch check, then /pm-plan (or /pm-fixup if trivial) -> pm-coder -> review loop -> pm-pr-prep. The orchestrator does not edit content artifacts (code, schemas, docs/architecture, plans, contracts) directly -- spawn the owning pm-* agent. Do not load wb-* role skills (coder / plan-feature / design-review / code-reviewer / pr-author / workflow) for code/PR/commit work; use the pm-* equivalents. code-review, deep-research and wb knowledge skills (codestyle, packaging, platform) stay available and should be used when relevant. Exception: a PM-authorized diagnostic probe (runtime/local, to test a hypothesis) is a throwaway spike, not a feature -- do not route it through /pm-plan; propose it in plain language with before->after and get an explicit yes first (WORKFLOW.md Step A.5). Surface substantive PM decision-forks (scope, accept-vs-fix, which-of-N, prioritization) via the AskUserQuestion tool, not plain prose; simple yes/no proceed-gates may stay prose."}}'
